Fine-Tuning, Training Techniques & Optimization — avg: 61 (Innovative)

These papers present complementary optimization strategies across machine learning and AI systems, addressing training stability, algorithmic efficiency, and inference robustness. P035 and P182 both tackle stability issues through regularization and correction: P035 introduces representation regularization during domain-adaptive finetuning to suppress task-induced shifts in embeddings, while P182 applies inference-time correction to mitigate bias in diffusion models. P093 and P122 target optimization of autonomous systems through different mechanisms: P093 uses genetic algorithms to evolve optimal agent initialization states for code generation, whereas P122 predicts inference failures and dynamically migrates computation from cheaper to more capable models in LLM-based agents. P084 contributes an algorithmic optimization approach by introducing an overlapping decomposition formulation for virtual network embedding. At a higher level, P145 provides crucial theoretical grounding by characterizing failure modes in large models through the Proxy Compression Hypothesis and establishing a taxonomy of exploit mechanisms—essential knowledge for developing robust optimization and mitigation strategies. Together, these papers illustrate that optimization in modern systems requires both domain-specific techniques and fundamental understanding of failure mechanisms.
  • P182 — Elucidating the SNR-t Bias of Diffusion Probabilistic Models [#11, Highly Innovative]
  • P122 — ATROPOS: Improving Cost-Benefit Trade-off of LLM-based Agents under Self-Consistency with Early Termination and Model Hotswap [#82, Innovative]
  • P035 — REZE: Representation Regularization for Domain-adaptive Text Embedding Pre-finetuning [#98, Innovative]
  • P084 — Overlapping decompositions of Virtual Network Embedding [#113, Moderately Innovative]
  • P093 — AgentGA: Evolving Code Solutions in Agent-Seed Space [#124, Moderately Innovative]
  • P145 — Reward Hacking in the Era of Large Models: Mechanisms, Emergent Misalignment, Challenges [#143, Moderately Innovative]

Agent Orchestration & Control — avg: 61 (Innovative)

P050 (AUTOPOIESIS) replaces static scheduling with LLM-evolved code for serving optimization. P072 (Scepsy) jointly optimizes GPU allocation and tensor parallelism for multi-LLM workflows. P092 (TREX) automates the entire LLM fine-tuning lifecycle via multi-agent MCTS exploration. P153 (AgentSPEX) provides a YAML DSL for explicit agent control flow. These systems shift from manual configuration toward adaptive, agent-driven orchestration.
  • P072 — Scepsy: Serving Agentic Workflows Using Aggregate LLM Pipelines [#55, Innovative]
  • P050 — AUTOPOIESIS: A Self-Evolving System Paradigm for LLM Serving Under Runtime Dynamics [#90, Innovative]
  • P153 — AgentSPEX: An Agent SPecification and EXecution Language [#110, Moderately Innovative]
  • P092 — TREX: Automating LLM Fine-tuning via Agent-Driven Tree-based Exploration [#121, Moderately Innovative]

Multimodal Omnimodal Models — avg: 61 (Innovative)

P139 (Omni) unifies text, images, videos, 3D, and hidden representations in a 3B-active MoE via Context Unrolling. P144 (LLaDA2.0-Uni) unifies understanding, image generation, and editing under block-level masked diffusion. P183 (Qwen3.5-Omni) achieves real-time streaming speech generation, agentic tools, and zero-shot voice cloning across 29 languages. These three papers represent the frontier of unified modality handling—each taking a different architectural approach (MoE, diffusion, streaming).
  • P144 — LLaDA2.0-Uni: Unifying Multimodal Understanding and Generation with Diffusion Large Language Model [#63, Innovative]
  • P139 — Context Unrolling in Omni Models [#114, Moderately Innovative]
  • P183 — Qwen3.5-Omni Technical Report [#115, Moderately Innovative]

Privacy, Security & Adversarial Robustness — avg: 60 (Innovative)

This cluster addresses overlapping security and robustness concerns in modern AI systems. P046 and P053 both employ Secure Multi-Party Computation (MPC) to protect model inference and routing, with P046 using encrypted routing for efficient secure transformer inference while P053 extends privacy guarantees to embedding-based LLM routing without sacrificing quality. However, P089 shifts focus from cryptographic protection to detecting systemic vulnerabilities—specifically, gender bias propagation in GPT-5 recruitment tasks—revealing how privacy-preserving systems may still encode harmful stereotypes. P179 takes yet another angle, introducing data-free and optimization-free attacks that degrade DNNs through targeted sign-bit flips on high-magnitude weights, demonstrating that even sophisticated neural networks remain vulnerable to low-complexity adversarial perturbations. Collectively, these papers suggest that securing AI systems requires a multi-faceted approach: defending against direct attacks (P179), protecting user privacy during inference and routing (P046, P053), and auditing model outputs for hidden biases (P089).
  • P179 — Maximal Brain Damage Without Data or Optimization: Disrupting Neural Networks via Sign-Bit Flips [#5, Highly Innovative]
  • P046 — SecureRouter: Encrypted Routing for Efficient Secure Inference [#96, Innovative]
  • P053 — Privacy-Preserving LLMs Routing [#100, Innovative]
  • P089 — Gender bias and propagation of stereotypes in GenAI-assisted recruitment [#161, Moderately Innovative]

Attention Mechanisms & Efficiency — avg: 60 (Innovative)

P051 (LoSA) applies sparse prefix attention for diffusion LMs. P056 (Ragged Paged Attention) fuses KV operations on TPU. P057 (LAAR) routes based on context length and accuracy. P063 (DepCap) adaptively sets diffusion block boundaries. These papers show how attention patterns and request routing can be tuned for both efficiency and quality in modern LLM serving.
  • P056 — Ragged Paged Attention: A High-Performance and Flexible LLM Inference Kernel for TPU [#38, Innovative]
  • P051 — LoSA: Locality Aware Sparse Attention for Block-Wise Diffusion Language Models [#83, Innovative]
  • P063 — DepCap: Adaptive Block-Wise Parallel Decoding for Efficient Diffusion LM Inference [#84, Innovative]
  • P057 — Accuracy Is Speed: Towards Long-Context-Aware Routing for Distributed LLM Serving [#153, Moderately Innovative]

Graph Neural Networks & Algorithms — avg: 58 (Moderately Innovative)

P001 (DiffPart) uses GNN + Gumbel-Softmax for task partitioning. P003 (UnderGS) replaces adjacency matrices with temporal-cohesive stores. P027 trains sparse graph transformers distributedly. P043 surveys 200+ graph-LLM integration methods. P044 introduces proximity matrices for hypergraph mining. P055 (AgentGL) trains LLM agents on text-attributed graphs. P071 (GEN-Graph) combines PUM and PNM for graph DP. P085 (MorphGL) optimizes mini-batch preparation for GNN training. P095 (HALO-GNN) stabilizes temporal GNNs against noise. P099 enforces incidence constraints in GPU partitioning. The group spans representation, training efficiency, and temporal stability across graph domains.
  • P003 — Understanding Evolving Graph Structures for Large Discrete-Time Dynamic Graph Representation [#7, Highly Innovative]
  • P085 — Efficient GNN Training on Giant Graphs with Collective Batching and Scheduling [#12, Highly Innovative]
  • P055 — AgentGL: Towards Agentic Graph Learning with LLMs via Reinforcement Learning [#31, Innovative]
  • P044 — Hypergraph Mining via Proximity Matrix [#39, Innovative]
  • P071 — GEN-Graph: Heterogeneous PIM Accelerator for General Computational Patterns in Graph-based Dynamic Programming [#52, Innovative]
  • P027 — Scalable and Adaptive Parallel Training of Graph Transformer on Large Graphs [#102, Innovative]
  • P099 — Incidence Constraints in Hypergraph Partitioning on GPU [#103, Innovative]
  • P001 — A Differentiable Approach to Task Graph Partitioning: A Case Study in RTL Simulation [#128, Moderately Innovative]
  • P043 — Integrating Graphs, Large Language Models, and Agents: Reasoning and Retrieval [#170, Incremental]
  • P095 — HALO-GNN: Hallucination-Resistant Temporal Graph Neural Networks for Dynamic Community Detection [#177, Routine]

Tabular Data Synthesis & Quality — avg: 58 (Moderately Innovative)

P040 (RDDG) synthesizes minority-class tabular data via self-reinforcing Bayesian calibration. P041 (PLAG) uses pseudo-labels and ECDF rarity scores for anomaly synthesis. P042 (TabGRAA) iteratively improves generation quality via group-relative advantage alignment. P096 (BSTabDiff) partitions high-dimensional features into latent blocks for diffusion. P097 measures behavioral fidelity in synthetic fraud patterns, showing most generators fail to preserve temporal and velocity signals. The group reveals synthesis quality as multi-faceted—requiring both label guidance and behavioral grounding.
  • P042 — Self-Improving Tabular Language Models via Iterative Group Alignment [#69, Innovative]
  • P097 — Synthetic Tabular Generators Fail to Preserve Behavioral Fraud Patterns: A Benchmark on Temporal, Velocity, and Multi-Account Signals [#70, Innovative]
  • P096 — BSTabDiff: Block-Subunit Diffusion Priors for High-Dimensional Tabular Data Generation [#86, Innovative]
  • P041 — Enhancing Tabular Anomaly Detection via Pseudo-Label-Guided Generation [#139, Moderately Innovative]
  • P040 — Self-Reinforcing Controllable Synthesis of Rare Relational Data via Bayesian Calibration [#146, Moderately Innovative]

LLM Interpretability & Mechanisms — avg: 58 (Moderately Innovative)

P015 (Implicit Curriculum) shows LLMs acquire skills in stable, compositional order readable from internals across model families. P016 demonstrates LLMs causally use confidence signals to govern abstention via steering and computational modeling. P048 (CPO++) extends DPO with domain-knowledge-guided counterfactuals for stable reasoning. P061 proposes a neural-symbolic parser using Minimalist Grammar and psycholinguistic priors for syntactic disambiguation. P083 (Learning ASCII) trains LLMs to construct spatial layouts, improving reasoning. P110 (SignThought) routes sign-video evidence through latent thought slots for grounded decoding. P127 (Cognitive Training) derives a meta-objective for curriculum discovery via Cross-Entropy Games. The group collectively moves from passive analysis toward active mechanism design and intervention.
  • P015 — What Do Language Models Learn and When? The Implicit Curriculum Hypothesis [#26, Innovative]
  • P016 — Causal Evidence that Language Models use Confidence to Drive Behavior [#27, Innovative]
  • P083 — Learning to Draw ASCII Improves Spatial Reasoning in Language Models [#72, Innovative]
  • P110 — Think in Latent Thoughts: A New Paradigm for Gloss-Free Sign Language Translation [#91, Innovative]
  • P127 — Cognitive Training for Language Models: Towards General Capabilities via Cross-Entropy Games [#122, Moderately Innovative]
  • P048 — Towards Robust Endogenous Reasoning: Unifying Drift Adaptation in Non-Stationary Tuning [#137, Moderately Innovative]
  • P061 — A Cognitively Plausible Neural-Symbolic Model for Syntactic Disambiguation in Minimalist Grammar [#171, Incremental]

Robotics & Embodied AI Control — avg: 58 (Moderately Innovative)

The robotics and embodied AI control papers in this cluster reflect complementary approaches to enabling robust real-world agent deployment. P126 addresses a critical technical challenge in autonomous vehicle perception by proposing the Object Distance Correction Algorithm, which resolves corrupted depth-camera estimates through sensor fusion with LiDAR consistency gates, demonstrating how specialized algorithmic interventions can enhance system reliability in safety-critical domains. In contrast, P170 takes a more expansive approach, introducing Agent-World as a large-scale training arena that synthesizes diverse real-world environments to develop general-purpose agents capable of handling complex, variable conditions through iterative reinforcement learning and automated diagnostic frameworks. While P126 focuses on mitigating specific sensor degradation at inference time, P170 emphasizes breadth of training experience as the path to generalization and robustness. Collectively, these papers suggest the field is converging on a two-pronged strategy: developing robust perception and fault-tolerance mechanisms for safety-critical systems like autonomous vehicles (P126), while simultaneously building scalable training paradigms that prepare agents for the unpredictability of real-world deployment (P170).
  • P170 — Agent-World: Scaling Real-World Environment Synthesis for Evolving General Agent Intelligence [#80, Innovative]
  • P126 — RACF: A Resilient Autonomous Car Framework with Object Distance Correction [#132, Moderately Innovative]

RAG with Evolving Knowledge Graphs — avg: 58 (Moderately Innovative)

P006 represents knowledge as order-aware hypergraphs for sequence inference. P017 (RRPO) optimizes RAG rerankers with downstream LLM feedback. P031 (EHRAG) combines structural and semantic hyperedges. P045 (EvoRAG) uses gradient-based feedback backpropagation to refine KG triplets. P186 (W-RAC) sends metadata instead of raw text to LLMs for chunking decisions. Collectively, they shift RAG from static retrieval toward adaptive, feedback-driven knowledge evolution.
  • P017 — Optimizing RAG Rerankers with LLM Feedback via Reinforcement Learning [#48, Innovative]
  • P045 — EvoRAG: Making Knowledge Graph-based RAG Automatically Evolve through Feedback-driven Backpropagation [#74, Innovative]
  • P031 — EHRAG: Bridging Semantic Gaps in Lightweight GraphRAG via Hybrid Hypergraph Construction and Retrieval [#89, Innovative]
  • P006 — Knowledge Is Not Static: Order-Aware Hypergraph RAG for Language Models [#125, Moderately Innovative]
  • P186 — Web Retrieval-Aware Chunking (W-RAC) for Efficient and Cost-Effective Retrieval-Augmented Generation Systems [#160, Moderately Innovative]

Video Understanding & Generation — avg: 58 (Moderately Innovative)

P060 reduces KV cache via Scene Change Detection for frame sampling. P068 (FFR) diagnoses failed video QA and injects evidence patches. P131 (WorldMark) benchmarks interactive video world models with 500 test cases. P133 introduces self-supervised speed estimation and control. P157 (CoInteract) jointly synthesizes RGB and texture-stripped HOI structure streams. P158 (AnyRecon) reconstructs 3D from arbitrary sparse views using video diffusion. P169 (MultiWorld) scales multi-agent video modeling with RoPE agent embeddings. The trend moves from passive understanding toward active control and synthesis of video content.
  • P068 — Find, Fix, Reason: Context Repair for Video Reasoning [#25, Innovative]
  • P133 — Seeing Fast and Slow: Learning the Flow of Time in Videos [#57, Innovative]
  • P158 — AnyRecon: Arbitrary-View 3D Reconstruction with Video Diffusion Model [#76, Innovative]
  • P169 — MultiWorld: Scalable Multi-Agent Multi-View Video World Models [#105, Innovative]
  • P131 — WorldMark: A Unified Benchmark Suite for Interactive Video World Models [#119, Moderately Innovative]
  • P157 — CoInteract: Physically-Consistent Human-Object Interaction Video Synthesis via Spatially-Structured Co-Generation [#138, Moderately Innovative]
  • P060 — Dealing with the Elephant in the KV Cache: Video Frame Sampling for Multimodal LLM Inference [#166, Incremental]

Time Series & Forecasting Models — avg: 57 (Moderately Innovative)

P070 shows TabPFN-TS exploits simple target-covariate dependencies far better than Chronos-2 at short horizons. P091 (Assessing Performance) benchmarks Moirai and ChronosX against task-specific models for probabilistic electricity price forecasting. P140 (LLATISA) introduces difficulty-stratified time series reasoning with curriculum training on verified CoT trajectories. These papers highlight the tradeoff between foundation models' generality and task-specific models' expressiveness, with curriculum learning enabling richer temporal reasoning.
  • P140 — LLATISA: Towards Difficulty-Stratified Time Series Reasoning from Visual Perception to Semantics [#58, Innovative]
  • P070 — Investigating Simple Target-Covariate Relationships for Chronos-2 and TabPFN-TS [#130, Moderately Innovative]
  • P091 — Assessing the Performance–Efficiency Trade-off of Foundation Models in Probabilistic Electricity Price Forecasting [#140, Moderately Innovative]

Training Curriculum & Dynamics — avg: 52 (Moderately Innovative)

These papers explore how to structure and control neural network training for improved learning dynamics. P064 leverages symbolic computation to guide training, using shallow neural networks as ansatz generators for PDEs, effectively creating a curriculum where mathematical constraints steer learning toward exact analytical solutions. P164 examines weight evolution during multi-task learning, proposing Task-Feature Specialization (TFS) to explain when task arithmetic succeeds and introducing OrthoReg to enforce disentangled, orthogonal weight updates. Although P064 constrains learning through architecture and symbolic integration while P164 does so through regularization, both papers demonstrate that imposing structure on neural network training—whether via problem formulation or weight constraints—yields more controlled and interpretable training dynamics. The collective insight suggests that explicitly guiding how networks adapt, through diverse mechanisms from symbolic computation to weight regularization, is key to advancing neural network training and generalization.
  • P164 — Understanding and Enforcing Weight Disentanglement in Task Arithmetic [#24, Innovative]
  • P064 — A new efficient symbolic computation fusion neural network method: Solving exact solutions of (3+1)-dimensional nonlinear partial differential equations [#168, Incremental]

Structured Data & Reasoning — avg: 51 (Moderately Innovative)

P002 diagnoses permutation invariance failures in table embeddings via CKA metrics. P005 (KumoRFM-2) performs in-context learning directly on relational databases without flattening. P033 (MACE) verifies claims against tabular evidence via multi-agent planning and bidirectional feedback. P098 (Fact4ac) fine-tunes Qwen for financial misinformation detection without external references. P101 (JCQL) jointly solves knowledge base completion and QA by coupling SLM as LLM agent action. P161 (Dual-View) generates contrastive pairs via polarity reversal for instruction-following retrieval. The group emphasizes structured understanding, consistency verification, and multi-task coupling across tabular domains.
  • P005 — KumoRFM-2: Scaling Foundation Models for Relational Learning [#8, Highly Innovative]
  • P161 — Dual-View Training for Instruction-Following Information Retrieval [#42, Innovative]
  • P002 — Towards Platonic Representation for Table Reasoning: A Foundation for Permutation-Invariant Retrieval [#147, Moderately Innovative]
  • P101 — Joint Knowledge Base Completion and Question Answering by Combining Large Language Models and Small Language Models [#148, Moderately Innovative]
  • P033 — A Multi-Agent Approach for Claim Verification from Tabular Data Documents [#155, Moderately Innovative]
  • P098 — Fact4ac at the Financial Misinformation Detection Challenge Task: Reference-Free Financial Misinformation Detection via Fine-Tuning and Few-Shot Prompting of Large Language Models [#175, Incremental]

KV Cache Memory Compression — avg: 51 (Moderately Innovative)

All four tackle KV cache bottlenecks in long-context inference. P022 (Green Decoding) co-optimizes speculative tokens and cache windows. P026 (HybridGen) distributes KV attention across CPU-GPU. P119 (Prefill-as-a-Service) offloads prefill to remote clusters. P120 (YOCO++) adds cross-layer residual connections. The trend spans inference optimization, distribution, and architectural rethinking of cache reuse.
  • P026 — HybridGen: Efficient LLM Generative Inference via CPU-GPU Hybrid Computing [#68, Innovative]
  • P120 — YOCO++: Enhancing YOCO with KV Residual Connections for Efficient LLM Inference [#117, Moderately Innovative]
  • P119 — Prefill-as-a-Service: KVCache of Next-Generation Models Could Go Cross-Datacenter [#127, Moderately Innovative]
  • P022 — Green Decoding: ELQ Co-Optimization and Carbon-Aware Scheduling for Efficient LLM Inference [#169, Incremental]

Practical Integration & Real-World Impact — avg: 48 (Moderately Innovative)

These four papers examine how AI must be integrated into broader real-world systems, constraints, and adoption contexts. P066 develops fair clustering algorithms simultaneously satisfying group-fairness and diverse-center constraints, integrating fairness principles directly into the approximation framework at polynomial-time cost. P077 builds a working talent recommendation system combining collaborative filtering and multi-criteria ranking, demonstrating the integration of algorithmic approaches into practical HR workflows. P138 deploys TingIS in production to discover incidents from 300,000 customer complaints daily at Alipay, reducing alert noise 94% while maintaining 95% critical-incident recall—solving the integration of noisy customer signals into operational monitoring. P113 analyzes Canadian enterprise AI adoption, finding that 78% of non-adopters cite irrelevance rather than prior dissatisfaction, revealing that successful integration requires matching AI capabilities to organizational needs and application domains. The collective insight is that technical capability alone is insufficient; real-world impact requires attending to fairness (P066), domain-specific workflows (P077), system-scale reliability (P138), and macro-level adoption alignment (P113).
  • P066 — Constant-Factor Approximations for Doubly Constrained Fair k-Center, k-Median and k-Means [#37, Innovative]
  • P138 — TingIS: Real-time Risk Event Discovery from Noisy Customer Incidents at Enterprise Scale [#47, Innovative]
  • P113 — From Hype to Output: How AI Investment Translates to Real Productivity Gains [#164, Incremental]
  • P077 — Collaborative Filtering and TOPSIS-Based Talent Scout Recommendation System [#179, Routine]

Low-Resource & Efficient Adaptation — avg: 46 (Moderately Innovative)

P034 (QU-NLP) achieves 90% on Arabic Islamic inheritance via QLoRA on Qwen3-4B on consumer hardware. P100 shows data augmentation effectiveness is governed by task structure, not LLM quality, for African language NLP. P125 (HUOZIIME) fine-tunes Qwen3-0.6B for on-device Chinese input with three-level hierarchical memory. P147 (TACO) automatically discovers and reuses observation compression rules for terminal agents. The group collectively addresses constraints of compute, memory, and data availability through targeted adaptation strategies.
  • P147 — A Self-Evolving Framework for Efficient Terminal Agents via Observational Context Compression [#97, Innovative]
  • P100 — When Does Data Augmentation Help? Evaluating LLM and Back-Translation Methods for Hausa and Fongbe NLP [#157, Moderately Innovative]
  • P125 — HUOZIIME: An On-Device LLM-enhanced Input Method for Deep Personalization [#159, Moderately Innovative]
  • P034 — QU-NLP at QIAS 2026: Multi-Stage QLoRA Fine-Tuning for Arabic Islamic Inheritance Reasoning [#165, Incremental]

Domain-Specific Applications — avg: 44 (Moderately Innovative)

P020 (KAMI) proposes six-layer architecture integrating KGs, neurosymbolic AI, GNNs for smart manufacturing. P062 proposes conditional process model for GenAI's impact on management accounting in manufacturing. P065 demonstrates protein-polymer conjugates for identifying surface-accessible binding sites via MS/MS. P080 (Framework-Constrained Materials) fixes structural framework while learning chemistry assignment for inorganic crystals. P108 reviews innate immune activation cascade in neuropsychiatric lupus. P130 (StyleID) calibrates facial identity recognition against stylization via human-perception metrics. P154 (Tstars-Tryon) supports multi-garment outfit composition with near-realtime inference on H200. These papers span manufacturing, accounting, biochemistry, materials science, medicine, and fashion—showing AI's broad applicability.

  • P154 — Tstars-Tryon 1.0: Robust and Realistic Virtual Try-On for Diverse Fashion Items [#107, Innovative]

Open-Ended Agent Research & Reasoning — avg: 44 (Moderately Innovative)

Both P008 and P128 survey critical enhancements to LLM capabilities, but from complementary perspectives: P008 catalogs methods for integrating graph structures into LLMs across input, model, and output phases through its Graph4LLM taxonomy, while P128 proposes a contextual cognition framework that explains LLM-based agent reasoning through four core processes—encoding, perception, interaction, and reasoning. Where P008 emphasizes architectural techniques—showing how graph integration improves performance across seven application domains—P128 takes a broader view of agent intelligence, focusing on how agents dynamically integrate internal LLM state with external observations to enable sophisticated decision-making. These approaches are complementary rather than competing: P008's graph-enhanced architectures could serve as one mechanism within P128's contextual cognition framework, enabling richer perception and more effective reasoning. Together, these surveys articulate a clear field-wide consensus that effective LLM agents require both structured knowledge integration (P008) and cognitive frameworks that model dynamic context awareness (P128). The collective trend suggests that advancing LLM-based systems will increasingly rely on combining graph-augmented architectures with agent designs that systematically encode, perceive, interact with, and reason about their environments.
  • P008 — Are Graphs Useful for LLMs? A Comprehensive Survey of Graph-Enhanced Large Language Models [#152, Moderately Innovative]
  • P128 — A Comprehensive Survey of the LLM-Based Agent: The Contextual Cognition Perspective [#158, Moderately Innovative]

Recommendation Systems & Personalization — avg: 44 (Moderately Innovative)

P037 estimates causal effects of AI recommendations on retention via DID + PSM across 2.4M customers. P069 (SEDIRec) generates contrastive user views via user-KG and diffusion for recommendation. P102 (Policy-Guided Simulation) anchors LLM reasoning and gradient-boosting via behavioral policies for merchant diagnostics. P106 (GenRec) deploys generative retrieval with GRPO alignment on production JD.com feeds. P107 (Early-Warning) predicts MOOC satisfaction from early-stage multi-modal signals. P178 (PersonaVLM) co-trains memory, personality evolution, and multi-step planning for long-term personalization. The group spans causal evaluation, heterogeneous data fusion, and long-term adaptation.
  • P106 — GenRec: A Preference-Oriented Generative Framework for Large-Scale Recommendation [#62, Innovative]
  • P178 — PersonaVLM: Long-Term Personalized Multimodal LLMs [#88, Innovative]
  • P069 — Large Language Models-Enhanced Semantic Diffusion for User-Centric Recommendation [#120, Moderately Innovative]
  • P102 — Meituan Merchant Business Diagnosis via Policy-Guided Dual-Process User Simulation [#126, Moderately Innovative]
  • P107 — Early-Warning Learner Satisfaction Forecasting in MOOCs via Temporal Event Transformers and LLM Text Embeddings [#178, Routine]
  • P037 — Effect of Personalised AI-Driven Recommendations on Customer Retention in E-Commerce Platforms [#184, Routine]

Multimodal & Interpretable Learning — avg: 38 (Incremental)

Both P086 and P078 integrate complementary modalities to build more robust and interpretable systems. P086 fuses student interaction sequences with facial expression features via asymmetric cross-attention, where behavioral state gates which affective signals matter, operationalizing the psychological principle that cognition selectively attends to emotion; the fusion improves knowledge tracing AUC by 7-12% on three datasets. P078 combines U-Net semantic segmentation, symbolic rule induction grounded in Core Knowledge theory, and DQN reinforcement learning to extract interpretable world models from arcade game frames; the event-chain causal reward enables generalization to visual changes without retraining. The trend across both is that single-modality systems miss key signals; structured integration of complementary sources—behavioral and affective, visual and symbolic and causal—yields both accuracy and interpretability impossible with any single modality alone.
  • P086 — MCA-KT: An expression-behaviour multimodal knowledge tracking model based on cross-attention [#136, Moderately Innovative]
  • P078 — Design and Development of a Framework for Extracting Interpretable Symbolic Knowledge from Video Streams Exploiting Core Knowledge [#174, Incremental]

Semantic Web & Knowledge Graphs — avg: 29 (Incremental)

P024 introduces OWL 2 DL for cyber range semantics. P025 proposes hybrid LoRA + RAG + KG alignment for domain knowledge extraction. P073-P076, P079, P081 are claims about semantic enrichment benefits (precision, engagement, retrieval). P082 surveys KG integration with AAS and OPC UA for Digital Twins. P111 extends the Oral Health ontology with composition-based material definitions. P116 (SMIR-BD) integrates OWL 2 DL and SPARQL for Bangladeshi repositories. P117 proposes Graph-LLM integration for clinical reasoning. P118 (SGDSS-BR) combines 563-concept municipality ontology with semantic analytics. P121 (COMED) enriches medical KGs with LLM-generated node descriptions. Together they demonstrate ontologies and knowledge graphs as enabling infrastructure across diverse domains.

  • P117 — No Man Is an Island: Explainable Graph-LLM Agents for Real-Time Clinical Reasoning [#162, Incremental]
  • P118 — Knowledge-Based Decision Support for E-Governance: A Semantic Analytics Platform for Public Service Delivery Optimization in Brazilian Municipal Governments [#172, Incremental]
  • P025 — Semantic Understanding and Knowledge Discovery with Large Language Models in Domain Corpora [#180, Routine]

Theory, Philosophy & Foundations — avg: 24 (Incremental)

The papers in this group represent a fascinating intersection of computational rigor and philosophical depth. P114 (S-AI-Solver) approaches problem-solving through a biologically-inspired numerical framework, using 'hormonal' signals to regulate an iterative solver across diverse equation classes, while P115 (Beyond Structure) tackles the foundational question of meaning itself, arguing that understanding cannot be reduced to structural description alone but must encompass five irreducible epistemic modes. While P114 focuses on algorithmic solutions to formally intractable problems, P115 critiques the limitation of purely structural approaches in philosophy and cognition. Together, these papers suggest a growing recognition in both computational and philosophical communities that effective problem-solving and understanding require frameworks that go beyond reductionist approaches—whether by incorporating regulatory mechanisms in algorithms or by acknowledging multiple epistemic aspects in conceptual analysis. This pairing indicates that future advances in AI and foundational theory may require bridging the gap between computational methods and philosophical rigor.
  • P115 — Beyond Structure: A Five-Aspect Model of Meaning, Sensibility, and Epistemic Determination [#173, Incremental]
  • P114 — S-AI-Solver: Toward Numerical Artificial Intelligence — A Hormonally Regulated Sparse AI Architecture for the Formally Certified Resolution of Analytically Intractable Equations [#176, Incremental]
